name: Emilia-Romagna (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the stylised regional emblem, evoking the verdant Po Valley
the stylised regional emblem, evoking the typical red roofs and bricks of the region's historic cities

description: A white field charged at the centre with the modern regional logo of Emilia-Romagna, a stylised flower-like composition rendered in green and red.
history: The contemporary regional flag was adopted in line with Regional Law n. 13 of 26 September 1995 and the regional graphic identity manual that defined the modern logo. The flag distinguishes the unified administrative region created in 1948 from the historic territories of Emilia (the Po Valley west of Bologna) and Romagna (around Ravenna and Forli).

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the regional logo, a stylised composition combining floral and geometric motifs in green, white and red.

features.role: stylised regional logo (a flower-like composition of green and red elements)
features.symbolism: The regional logo, designed in the 1990s, evokes the rich flora of the Po Valley and the unity of the historically distinct lands of Emilia and Romagna.
